content:
Hello everyone. I am planning to write a driver for a power monitor (pac1921) in rust. I would start from the @Fabien Parent 's branch (mentioned here) since it already has most of the necessary abstractions: i2c, regmap and of. On top of that I would try to add abstractions to hwmon.
A couple of questions:

  • Has anyone already tried to submit or discussed any rust based hwmon driver previously? This to have an idea about whether or not that would be considered by the hwmon mantainers.
  • @Dirk Behme you mentioned you picked the extension to read DT properties and included it in your work. Is it available in some of your branches? Are you planning to PR it to Fabien or to rust-for-linux?

Any suggestions would be appreciated.

@Dirk Behme you mentioned you picked the extension to read DT properties and included it in your work. Is it available in some of your branches? Are you planning to PR it to Fabien or to rust-for-linux?

I did a PR for Fabien :grinning: As the base seems to have been rebased, that PR looks broken, though. However, the relevant patch seems to be there, still :grinning_face_with_smiling_eyes: Please try that. As the base versions change frequently, let me know in case you can't get it applied. We can have a look, then.

It seems Fabien doesn't take it to keep his code simple and to not add even more dependencies. What is totally fine :smiley:
Thanks @Dirk Behme ! I confirm I could apply the commit and can read a DT property.

I've just found out that a driver for a similar chip is already existing under iio, so I will likely extend that driver or create a new one based on that.
However I think I've got some familiarity with rust-for-linux thus if there is any help needed in the device driver area I could give it a try. Of course I will keep an eye on the issue list as well.

Matteo Martelli 說：

undefined

I saw the discussion about hwmon abstractions from a while ago. I'm currently working on adding MECHREVO WUJIE series support to the uniwill-acpi driver (v2 patch set just sent to platform-x86), and I'm very interested in pushing the Rust hwmon abstractions forward as a next step.
My plan is to start with a minimal MVP—supporting just temperature sensors and read-only operations to keep the initial abstraction clean and reviewable. Has anyone made any (hidden) progress on this since 2024? I’d love to sync up or build upon existing work if there’s any.
I am not aware of any progress, but I also would need hwmon in rust for my driver eventually. If submitting hwmon patches, please add me "Markus Probst <markus.probst@posteo.de>" to Cc.
